Notes: Went for the redpoint but I was too spent, haven't been able to try hard often enough lately :( It'll go soon though!

Finally found beta for after the pinky fingerlock! Work the feet up, left hand to a sloper, bump the right hand to the good crimp, bump the left hand to the good hold below the bolt, work the feet up on the tiny slopers, keep those hips in and shoot for the jug with the right. Home free!!
Lead the little known third pitch that I've been eyeing from the belay ledge forever! An underrated lead, it was pretty fun, with a SUPER nice belay spot from the top.

Partner(s): Yav Pe., Tom Ce., Erica Be.
Notes: Took a shot at some of Tom’s beta here, but didn’t really like it. Going out right for some side pulls

Partner(s): Yav Pe., Tom Ce., Erica Be.
Yav Pe., Tom Ce., Erica Be.
Notes: Really fun climb! Linked pitches 1 and 2, don’t know why you’d do any different.
Figured out that clipping the crux bolt after pulling the crux is the way to go!
Send go but botched it right below the last bolt :( Gotta refine my redpoint crux beta and smooth out my beta low down to save more energy
Bolt to bolt to warm up. Feeling great about my crux beta!
So close againnnnn. Was pretty tired and started feeling it at the second bolt but persisted all the way through the crux but punted before clipping the last bolt. It'll go next time!
Didn���t work my right foot up before going for the first undercling. So close!
Found the crux beta! Get the secret undercling under the other undercling with the left hand to reach the pinkie finger lock!
Yav led up to the first crux right below the big ledge, then I TRd to that point and led the rest to the anchors
Climbed smooth and confidently which saw me through. Excited for another lead attempt!
Went left at the crux and fell, then went right and it went! Took at a couple more bolts because I already biffed the onsight.

Partner(s): Yav Pe., Arthur Br., Chad Co.
Notes: Training with Chad Arthur and Yav. Yav led a short pitch up the toe and I finished it to the normal top of p1. Arthur did the next and Yav the final pitch.
Trd to a few moves below the crux then led the rest
